evals.prompt: Create a basic Spring Boot project named 'user-manager' using Java 25 and Spring Boot 4.1.0 with controllers, services, repositories, and models for a User entity. The User entity should have id, username, email, and password (which is sensitive). Make sure to use record for DTOs, application.properties, a mapper class, and include the Maven Wrapper.
evals.expected_output: A fully functional user-manager Spring Boot project structure with a REST endpoint for Users, using DTO records, mapper class, layered architecture, application.properties, and Maven Wrapper.
expectations: The project uses Java version 25 and Spring Boot version 4.1.0 or higher in pom.xml.
Maven Wrapper files (.mvn directory, mvnw, and mvnw.cmd) exist in the root of the project.
The project uses application.properties for configuration, and no application.yml or application.yaml files are present.
The UserDto is defined as a record and does not contain sensitive fields (password, createdAt, updatedAt, createAt, updateAt).
The UserRepository is an interface that extends JpaRepository.
A UserMapper class exists to explicitly map between User entity and UserDto in both directions.
UserController delegates to UserService, and only exposes REST endpoints returning DTO records.

evals.prompt: Initialize a Spring Boot project named 'bookstore' (Java 25, Spring Boot 4.1.0) and build a Thymeleaf web monolith for displaying books. Include a reusable base layout with header, main, and footer (centered content), Tailwind CSS configuration, and a Book controller, service, repository, and mapper. Exclude sensitive fields from the Book DTO record.
evals.expected_output: A bookstore Spring Boot project using Thymeleaf with a layout structure (base.html, header.html, footer.html) containing semantic HTML, plus Book model, repository, service with mapper, controller, and DTO records.
expectations: The project uses Java version 25 and Spring Boot version 4.1.0 or higher in pom.xml.
Maven Wrapper files (.mvn directory, mvnw, and mvnw.cmd) exist in the root of the project.
The UI structure includes Thymeleaf templates layouts/base.html, fragments/header.html, and fragments/footer.html.
The Thymeleaf views utilize semantic HTML5 elements including header, main, and footer.
The BookDto is defined as a record and does not contain sensitive fields (password, createdAt, updatedAt, createAt, updateAt).
A BookMapper class exists to explicitly map between Book entity and BookDto in both directions.
The repository layer extends JpaRepository for access.
